thesis: Advanced nuclear technology company developing the Integral Molten Salt Reactor (IMSR) — a Generation IV small modular reactor using Standard-Assay Low Enriched Uranium (SALEU) fuel. First molten salt reactor to complete CNSC Vendor Design Review and receive NRC Safety Evaluation. Pre-revenue with ~4-5 year cash runway post-SPAC. TETRA pilot reactor scheduled for criticality July 2026 under DOE fast-track program.

drivers: TETRA pilot reactor achieves criticality on schedule (July 2026)
NRC continues timely review of design elements
First Construction Permit application submitted by 2028
3-4 plants operational by 2035 with recurring core replacement revenues

risks: NRC raises unforeseen safety concerns with molten salt design
TETRA pilot reactor encounters technical issues
Funding dries up; dilutive raises required
First plant delayed to 2040+ or cancelled

base_case.assumptions: 12-18 month delays in regulatory timeline; 2-3 plants operational by 2036; Moderate competition from NuScale, Kairos; One additional capital raise required; Revenue of $300M by 2036.

catalysts.event: TETRA Reactor Criticality — DOE fast-track program milestone
Additional NRC Topical Report Approvals — Further de-risks licensing
Site Characterization Completion (Texas A&M) — Enables Construction Permit
First Construction Permit Application — Major regulatory milestone

key_risks: Pre-revenue with no near-term revenue expected until ~2030
Regulatory delays — NRC has never licensed a non-water-cooled commercial reactor
Dilution risk from future capital raises before commercialization
Technology execution risk — first-of-a-kind reactor deployment
80% decline from SPAC highs indicates market skepticism
